Metadata.defaultTitle: Model UN & Academic Competitions for Grades 5-8 | JAMUN
Metadata.defaultDescription: Affordable academic competition programs for middle school students (grades 5-8). Build public speaking, critical thinking, debate, and leadership skills through Model UN, Mock Trial, and Mathletes competitions. Grants available.

StructuredData.modelUNProgramDescription: Learn diplomatic skills, global issues analysis, public speaking, and resolution writing through our Model UN program designed for middle school students in grades 5-8.
StructuredData.mockTrialProgramDescription: Learn legal reasoning, cross-examination, opening statements, and persuasive argumentation through courtroom simulation designed for middle school students in grades 5-8.
StructuredData.mathletesProgramDescription: Compete in JAMUN's own math competitions through team-based competitive math training designed for middle school students in grades 5-8. Great preparation for MATHCOUNTS and AMC 8.

hero.description: JAMUN (Junior Assembly of the Model United Nations) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization run entirely by passionate high school and college student volunteers. We provide Model UN, Mock Trial, and Mathletes programs for middle schoolers — with free resources, low-cost conferences, and grants for those who need them — because we believe academic competition should be exciting, accessible, and, most importantly, {fun}.

mission.paragraph1: We believe every middle school student deserves the chance to discover their potential through academic competition. Research shows that students who participate in Model UN, debate, Mock Trial, and math competitions develop stronger public speaking, critical thinking, and leadership skills — abilities that transfer directly to academic success and college readiness.
mission.paragraph2: JAMUN exists to make these transformative experiences accessible to all. We provide free and low-cost programs in Model UN (diplomatic simulation), Mock Trial (courtroom advocacy), and Mathletes (competitive mathematics) — designed specifically for students in grades 5–8. Our goal isn’t just to teach skills; it’s to spark a lifelong love of learning that prepares students for magnet schools, high school honors programs, and college admissions.
mission.blockquote: We’re not just running tournaments. We’re showing kids that being smart is cool, that hard work pays off, and that they can accomplish incredible things.

journey.headingAccent: why is this so hard to access?
journey.subtitle: JAMUN began as a frustration. Conferences cost hundreds of dollars. Prep materials sat behind paywalls. The schools that needed these programs the most couldn’t get in the door. So we built the version we wished had existed when we were eleven — and here’s how it’s gone since.

entry1.title: The spark.
entry1.body: JAMUN was founded by a small group of high school students who saw a gap: middle schoolers were being left behind in academic competitions. We decided to change that.

entry2.title: Growing roots.
entry2.body: Our first conferences took flight. What started as a single Model UN event expanded to include Mock Trial and Mathletes, reaching students across multiple states.

entry3.title: Building momentum.
entry3.body: JAMUN earned 501(c)(3) nonprofit status. Our volunteer base grew to 80+, and we raised over $70,000 to make programs accessible to all.

entry4.title: Looking forward.
entry4.body: With 500+ students impacted and 30+ schools reached, we’re just getting started. Our mission remains the same: make academics fun for every student.

stats.heading: Our impact, so far.
stats.subtitle: Every number represents a student inspired, a school empowered, or a volunteer who gave their time to make a difference.

values.heading: Our core values.
values.subtitle: Four principles that guide everything we do at JAMUN — from which conferences we run to which families get grants.

passionDriven.title: Passion-driven.
passionDriven.body: We believe that genuine enthusiasm is contagious. Our volunteers bring energy and excitement that transforms academic competition into an adventure.

communityFirst.title: Community first.
communityFirst.body: We’re building more than programs — we’re building a supportive family where every student feels they belong, regardless of experience level.

innovation.title: Innovation.
innovation.body: As a youth-led organization, we think differently. We constantly reimagine how to make learning engaging and competitions accessible.

excellence.title: Excellence.
excellence.body: We hold ourselves to the highest standards because our students deserve nothing less. Quality in everything we create.

testimonial.heading: Volunteers who actually show up.
testimonial.quote: This was something they were passionate about, and they put in an extraordinary amount of time and care into putting together a learning experience our students will not forget.
testimonial.attribution: — Mr. Komie and Ms. Mastin, Middle School Social Studies Teachers

team.heading: The people behind JAMUN.
team.subtitle: A dedicated group of student leaders working to make academics accessible and exciting — backed by a deep bench of volunteers.

joshuaVaron.name: Joshua Varon
joshuaVaron.bio: Student at Duke University studying mathematics and computer science. Founded JAMUN to make academic competition accessible to every middle schooler.

dustinSimon.name: Dustin Simon
dustinSimon.bio: Tennis enthusiast studying mathematics. Stewards JAMUN’s finances and grant fund so that every dollar reaches the students and programs it was meant for.

charlieFumerton.name: Charlie Fumerton
charlieFumerton.bio: Volleyball player with a keen interest in politics and international relations. Builds the curriculum, blog, and free resource library at the heart of JAMUN.

danyEsparza.name: Dany Esparza
danyEsparza.bio: Leads Spanish-language programming and translation so JAMUN’s resources reach students whose first language isn’t English.

bellaEsparza.name: Bella Esparza
bellaEsparza.bio: Works alongside Dany to expand JAMUN across Spanish-speaking schools and to keep the multilingual experience first-class.

jiahanLyu.name: Jiahan Lyu
jiahanLyu.bio: Develops JAMUN’s international outreach, bringing competitive academic programming to students beyond the U.S.

willBallis.name: Will Ballis
willBallis.bio: Former Mathletes champion who studied advanced mathematics. Connects JAMUN with the schools, donors, and partners that fund the grant program.

team.volunteersParagraph: Our leadership team is supported by {volunteerCount} who share our passion for empowering young learners. Together, we’re proving that students can create real, lasting change.

finalCta.description: Whether you’re a student ready to compete, a teacher looking to start a program, or someone who wants to support our mission — there’s a place for you at JAMUN.

faq.title: Common questions about donating.
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before making your gift.

taxDeductible.a: Yes. JAMUN is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization (EIN: 39-3081214). All donations are tax-deductible to the full extent allowed by law. You’ll receive a receipt for your records.

whereMoneyGoes.a: JAMUN is 100% volunteer-run, so every dollar goes directly to student programs. Your donation funds conference grants for families, training materials, competition resources, and program operations that make academic competitions accessible to middle school students across the country.

recurringDonation.q: Can I set up a recurring donation?
recurringDonation.a: Yes! Our donation platform supports one-time and recurring gifts. Monthly donations provide steady support that helps us plan programs, fund grants, and reach more students throughout the year.

employerMatch.q: Can my employer match my donation?
employerMatch.a: Many employers offer matching gift programs that double or even triple your contribution. Check with your HR department — you can often submit our EIN (39-3081214) through your company’s matching gift portal.

specificProgram.q: Can I donate to a specific program or school?
specificProgram.a: Absolutely. If you’d like to direct your gift toward a specific program (Model UN, Mock Trial, or Mathletes), a particular school, or the general grant fund, just include a note with your donation and we’ll honor your request.

howItWorks.title: A Simple Path to Funding.
howItWorks.subtitle: Our streamlined application process makes it easy to apply for the support you need.

step01.title: Submit Application
step01.body: Fill out our simple online form with your information and grant request details. It takes just 10–15 minutes.

step02.title: Review Process
step02.body: Our team reviews applications within 2–3 weeks and may reach out for additional information if needed.

step03.title: Receive Decision
step03.body: You’ll be notified of the decision via email with clear next steps if approved.

step04.title: Get Funded
step04.body: Approved grants are disbursed directly to cover your specified expenses. It’s that simple.

whatGrantsCover.title: Comprehensive Support for Your Journey.
whatGrantsCover.subtitle: Our grants are flexible and designed to cover the full range of expenses that might stand between a student and their chance to compete.

studentTestimonial.heading: Students find their voice.
studentTestimonial.quote: The JAMUN grant gave me the opportunity to attend my first Model UN conference. It was an experience that changed my perspective on the world.

faq.title: Common Questions About Our Grants.
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about applying for and receiving a JAMUN grant.

eligibility.q: Who is eligible for a JAMUN grant?
eligibility.a: Any middle school student in grades 5–8 who needs financial assistance to participate in Model UN conferences, Mock Trial competitions, or Mathletes tournaments is eligible to apply. Both individual students and school teams can request funding.

expenses.q: What expenses does the grant cover?
expenses.a: Grants can cover conference registration fees, travel and transportation costs, competition materials, training programs, team uniforms and supplies, and coaching resources. We work with each applicant to understand their unique needs.

timeline.q: How long does the application process take?
timeline.a: The application itself takes about 10–15 minutes to complete. Our team reviews all submissions within 2–3 weeks and will notify you of the decision via email.

distribution.a: Approved grants are disbursed directly to cover the specified expenses — for example, we may pay the conference registration fee on your behalf or reimburse documented costs. We never send unrestricted cash.

multiplePrograms.q: Can I apply for grants for multiple programs?
multiplePrograms.a: Yes! Students can apply for grants for any of JAMUN’s programs — Model UN, Mock Trial, and Mathletes. You can submit separate applications for different programs or events throughout the year.

finalCta.description: Fill out a short application and our team will review it within 2–3 weeks. Don’t let cost stand in the way of your potential.

LandingPage.whyCompetitionsTitle: Why academic competitions matter for middle schoolers.
LandingPage.whyCompetitionsBody1: Research shows that students who participate in academic competitions like Model UN, Mock Trial, and math leagues demonstrate stronger performance on standardized tests, higher GPAs, and better college admission rates. These extracurricular activities develop essential 21st-century skills that can't be taught in traditional classrooms alone.
LandingPage.whyCompetitionsBody2: Starting in middle school (grades 5–8) gives students a significant advantage. They build foundational skills in public speaking, debate, and critical thinking that compound over time, making them more competitive for magnet school programs, high school honors tracks, and eventually college admissions.
LandingPage.faqSubtitle: Everything families and educators ask before joining their first JAMUN conference.
LandingPage.faq1Q: What is Model UN, and how does it help middle schoolers?
LandingPage.faq1A: Model UN is an educational simulation where students represent different countries and debate global issues as UN delegates. For middle schoolers in grades 5–8, it builds public speaking, research, diplomatic negotiation, and an early understanding of international relations. Students learn to write position papers, draft resolutions, and deliver speeches — skills that compound into high school debate, magnet admissions, and college applications.
LandingPage.faq2Q: How do I prepare for my first Mock Trial competition?
LandingPage.faq2A: We provide free Mock Trial prep: case analysis guides, opening statement templates, cross-examination techniques, and practice sessions. New delegates learn courtroom procedure, evidence rules, and objection strategies through step-by-step tutorials. Our experienced mentors help students grow into both attorney and witness roles.
LandingPage.faq3Q: What math competitions does JAMUN offer?
LandingPage.faq3A: JAMUN hosts its own Mathletes math competitions where students tackle challenging problems in number theory, algebra, geometry, and problem-solving strategy. Students compete individually and as teams, building speed, accuracy, and creative mathematical thinking. Competing with JAMUN also helps prepare students for external competitions like MATHCOUNTS, AMC 8, and Math League.
LandingPage.faq4A: Yes. All of our curriculum guides, training materials, and resources are completely free. Conferences are low-cost, and our grant program covers up to 100% of conference costs — including registration, travel, and materials — for families who need support. As a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, every donation goes directly to student programs and the grant fund.
LandingPage.faq5Q: How can my school start a Model UN club or academic team?
LandingPage.faq5A: JAMUN provides complete startup kits: free curriculum, training, position paper templates, and ongoing mentorship for educators. Whether you're launching a Model UN club, Mock Trial team, or Mathletes program, we'll help you draft an implementation plan tailored to your school. Email us to schedule a consultation.
LandingPage.faq6Q: Do academic competitions help with high school and college admissions?
LandingPage.faq6A: Yes — academic competitions demonstrate intellectual curiosity, leadership, and commitment, the qualities admissions officers value most. Students who participate from middle school build impressive track records of public speaking, critical thinking, and teamwork that strengthen high school, magnet, and college applications.
LandingPage.faq7Q: Do students need experience to join?
LandingPage.faq7A: Not at all. JAMUN is designed for beginners. Most of our delegates start with zero experience and our coaches teach you everything — from how to research your country to parliamentary procedure to building a Mock Trial case. The only requirement is showing up curious.

hero.description: Compete in JAMUN's own math competitions for grades 5–8. Master problem-solving, build speed, and challenge yourself alongside peers. Great preparation for MATHCOUNTS and AMC 8 too. Launching Fall 2026.

whatIs.heading: A sport for your brain.
whatIs.paragraph1: Mathletes is a competitive mathematics program where students challenge themselves with problems that go beyond the classroom. Think of it as a sport for your brain — with individual events, team competitions, and the thrill of solving problems under pressure.
whatIs.paragraph2: It's not about memorizing formulas — it's about creative thinking, pattern recognition, and the satisfaction of cracking a tough problem. Whether you love puzzles or want to strengthen your math skills, Mathletes makes math genuinely exciting.

emeraldTestimonial.heading: Students learn to love the challenge.
emeraldTestimonial.quote: Math used to be just homework. Now it's my favorite puzzle to solve with friends. Getting stuck is just part of the process.

mathletesActivities.sectionSubtitle: Every mathlete follows the same path — from weekly practice to competition day. Here's what the experience looks like.
mathletesActivities.activity1Description: Master creative approaches to math problems that go beyond textbook methods. Discover elegant solutions.
mathletesActivities.activity2Description: Work through challenging problems with teammates and coaches, building speed and accuracy over time.
mathletesActivities.activity3Description: Collaborate with teammates in relay rounds and team challenges where every member contributes.
mathletesActivities.activity4Description: Compete for individual and team awards at regional and national-level competitions.

competitionFormat.heading: How competitions work.
competitionFormat.description: From sprint rounds to countdown brackets, here's what to expect at a typical competition.
competitionFormat.step1Body: Individual challenge: 30 problems in 40 minutes testing accuracy and speed across all math topics. No calculator allowed.
competitionFormat.step2Body: Pairs of challenging problems with 6 minutes each. Focus on deep problem-solving and mathematical reasoning over raw speed.
competitionFormat.step3Body: Work together with your team of 4 to solve 10 problems in 20 minutes. Communication and collaboration are key.
competitionFormat.step4Body: Head-to-head bracket tournament for top scorers. First to buzz in with the correct answer advances. The crowd favorite.

topicsSpread.heading: Topics we cover.
topicsSpread.footerText: Topics expand as you advance. Our curriculum grows with you from beginner to competition-ready.

skills.sectionSubtitle: Mathletes isn't just about math problems — it's about building skills that matter in school, career, and life.
skills.skill1Description: Develop systematic approaches to breaking down complex problems into manageable steps.
skills.skill2Description: Build lightning-fast calculation skills and number sense that serve you in all areas of academics.
skills.skill3Description: Learn to identify patterns, make predictions, and choose the best approach to each problem.
skills.skill4Description: Work with teammates to solve problems faster together than you could alone.

orangeTestimonial.heading: Parents see the confidence build.
orangeTestimonial.quote: Mathletes taught me that getting stuck is just part of the process. Now I actually enjoy the challenge of figuring things out.

resources.sectionSubtitle: From practice problem sets to strategy guides — everything you need to prepare for competition, at no cost.
resources.resource1Description: An introduction to competitive math and how to begin your Mathletes journey.
resources.resource2Description: Curated problems organized by difficulty and topic for daily practice.
resources.resource3Description: Essential formulas and theorems you'll need for competition success.
resources.resource4Description: Techniques for faster calculations and building number sense.
resources.resource5Description: Systematic approaches like working backwards and finding patterns.
resources.resource6Description: Real problems from previous competitions with detailed solutions.

faq.sectionSubtitle: Everything parents and students want to know about JAMUN Mathletes.
faq.q1: Do I need to be a math genius to join Mathletes?
faq.a1: Not at all! Mathletes is for students who enjoy math and want to challenge themselves. We welcome all skill levels and provide training that helps everyone improve. Some of our best competitors started with no competition experience.
faq.a2: JAMUN Mathletes is designed for middle school students in grades 5–8. We create age-appropriate practice materials and competition divisions so students compete with peers at similar levels.
faq.a3: Mathletes begins in Fall 2026! We're currently building our curriculum and partnerships. Join our mailing list to be the first to know when registration opens.
faq.q4: How much time does Mathletes require?
faq.a4: About 4 hours per week with meetings every two weeks and 4 tournaments per year. Students are encouraged to do additional individual practice, but the time commitment is flexible based on your goals.
faq.q5: What math level do I need?
faq.a5: If you're comfortable with pre-algebra concepts, you're ready to start! Our training covers topics from basic arithmetic through introductory algebra and geometry.
faq.q6: Is there a cost to participate?
faq.a6: We work hard to keep costs minimal. Many of our resources are FREE, and we offer financial assistance for competition registration fees. No student should be unable to participate due to cost.

finalCta.description: We're building a program where math is exciting, challenging, and fun. Whether you're a student ready to test your skills or a teacher looking to start a Mathletes club, join our interest list to be the first to know when we launch.

hero.description: JAMUN Mock Trial lets middle schoolers experience courtroom drama firsthand — building critical thinking, public speaking, and legal reasoning skills. Launching Fall 2026.

whatIs.heading: A courtroom simulation built for middle schoolers.
whatIs.paragraph1: Mock Trial is a competitive simulation where students take on the roles of attorneys and witnesses in a realistic courtroom setting. You analyze a case, prepare arguments, examine witnesses, and present your case to real judges.
whatIs.paragraph2: It's not about memorizing legal jargon — it's about critical thinking, teamwork, and the thrill of making your case under pressure. Whether you dream of being a lawyer or just love a good argument, Mock Trial is for you.

purpleTestimonial.heading: Students discover a voice they didn't know they had.
purpleTestimonial.quote: Mock Trial pushed me out of my comfort zone. Now I can think on my feet and argue my point confidently — in the courtroom and in class.

courtroomActivities.sectionTitle: From case file to closing argument.
courtroomActivities.sectionSubtitle: Every mock trial attorney and witness follows the same path. Here's what the courtroom experience looks like.
courtroomActivities.activity1Description: Study case materials, identify key facts, legal issues, and build your theory of the case from the ground up.
courtroomActivities.activity2Description: Craft compelling opening statements, closing arguments, and examination questions that tell your side's story.
courtroomActivities.activity3Description: Conduct direct and cross-examinations, drawing out testimony that supports your case and challenges the opposition.
courtroomActivities.activity4Description: Present your case to real attorneys and judges, respond to objections, and think on your feet under pressure.

trialFormat.heading: How a trial works.
trialFormat.description: From opening statements to the final verdict, here's what to expect in competition.
trialFormat.step1Body: Each side presents their theory of the case to the jury. This is your chance to frame the narrative and preview the evidence.
trialFormat.step2Body: Attorneys call witnesses for direct examination, then the opposing side cross-examines. This is where cases are won or lost.
trialFormat.step3Body: Attorneys raise objections based on the Rules of Evidence. Judges rule on each one. Knowing when and how to object is a crucial skill.
trialFormat.step4Body: Both sides summarize the evidence, highlight strengths in their case, and ask the jury to reach a verdict in their favor.

featuredCase.label: Fall 2026 Case
featuredCase.caseTypeBadge: Criminal Case — Murder in the First Degree
featuredCase.description1: When Ethan Morales, 22, is found dead in a Briarwood University chemistry lab, all evidence points to fellow student Alex Morgan. Was it sabotage — or a tragic accident?
featuredCase.description2: Teams will argue both sides using security footage, chemical evidence, and testimony from 6 witnesses per side. 12 exhibits. One verdict.

skills.sectionSubtitle: Mock Trial isn't just courtroom drama — it's about building skills that matter in school, college applications, and beyond.
skills.skill1Description: Learn to present arguments clearly, compellingly, and with the confidence to convince judges and juries.
skills.skill2Description: Break down complex situations, identify weaknesses in arguments, and develop airtight legal reasoning.
skills.skill3Description: Coordinate with teammates to build a cohesive case strategy where every role contributes to the outcome.
skills.skill4Description: Respond to unexpected objections, adapt your strategy mid-trial, and think clearly under pressure.

orangeTestimonial.heading: Parents see the transformation.
orangeTestimonial.quote: I never thought I'd love public speaking, but Mock Trial changed that. Now I'm the first one to volunteer for presentations in class.

resources.resource2Description: Quick reference guide for common objections and when to use them in trial.
resources.resource3Description: A structured template to help you craft a powerful opening statement.
resources.resource4Description: Learn effective strategies for questioning hostile witnesses on the stand.
resources.resource5Description: A student-friendly guide to the Federal Rules of Evidence used in competition.
resources.resource6Description: How to structure a closing argument that ties the evidence together convincingly.

faq.sectionSubtitle: Everything parents and students want to know about JAMUN Mock Trial.
faq.q1: Do I need any experience to join Mock Trial?
faq.a1: Not at all! Our program is designed specifically for beginners. We'll teach you everything from courtroom procedure to how to examine a witness. Many of our most confident advocates started with zero experience.
faq.a2: JAMUN Mock Trial is designed for middle school students in grades 5–8. We create age-appropriate cases and provide training that meets students where they are.
faq.a3: Mock Trial launches in Fall 2026! We're currently building our curriculum and case materials. Join our mailing list to be the first to know when registration opens.
faq.a4: You can play an attorney (prosecution or defense) or a witness. Most students get the opportunity to try both roles throughout the season, so you can find what suits you best.
faq.q5: How much time does Mock Trial require?
faq.a5: Mock Trial involves about 5 hours per week with team practices and individual preparation. Competitions are typically full-day events on weekends, with 2 competitions per year.
faq.q6: Is there a cost to participate?
faq.a6: We work hard to keep costs minimal. Many of our resources are FREE, and we offer financial assistance for competition fees. No student should be unable to participate due to cost.

finalCta.description: We're building a program where courtroom skills meet critical thinking. Whether you're a student ready to make your case or a teacher looking to start a Mock Trial club, join our interest list to be the first to know when we launch.

hero.headingPart1: Step into the shoes of a
hero.description: JAMUN Model UN gives middle schoolers the chance to debate real global issues, build critical skills, and discover their voice — no experience required.

whatIsMun.heading: A simulation of the United Nations.
whatIsMun.paragraph1: Model United Nations is an academic simulation where students role-play as delegates of UN member states. You research a real country, debate global topics, negotiate with peers, and draft resolutions — just like real diplomats do.
whatIsMun.paragraph2: JAMUN’s program is built specifically for grades 5–8 and designed so that even students with zero experience can thrive. Whether you’re passionate about climate change, human rights, or international security, there’s a committee for you.

blueTestimonial.heading: Delegates discover strengths they didn’t know they had.
blueTestimonial.quote: Model UN taught me how to speak up for what I believe in. I used to be terrified of public speaking, but after my first conference I couldn’t wait for the next one.
blueTestimonial.attribution: — A JAMUN delegate, on their first conference
blueTestimonial.photoAlt: JAMUN delegate listening intently during committee debate.

delegateActivities.heading: Four steps to diplomacy.
delegateActivities.subtitle: Every delegate follows the same path — from research to resolution. Here’s what a typical Model UN experience looks like.

researchYourCountry.title: Research Your Country
researchYourCountry.description: Dive deep into your assigned nation’s history, politics, and stance on global issues. Become the expert in the room.

writePositionPapers.title: Write Position Papers
writePositionPapers.description: Craft compelling documents that outline your country’s position and proposed solutions to world challenges.

deliverSpeeches.title: Deliver Speeches
deliverSpeeches.description: Take the floor to address the committee, articulate your positions, and respond to questions from other delegates.

buildCoalitions.title: Build Coalitions
buildCoalitions.description: Work with allies to draft resolutions, negotiate compromises, and build consensus on complex global issues.

conferenceDay.heading: What a conference day looks like.
conferenceDay.description: Conferences are typically 1–2 days on weekends. Here’s the rhythm of a typical JAMUN conference.

openingCeremonies.title: Opening Ceremonies
openingCeremonies.body: Conferences begin with inspiring speakers and orientation to set the tone for productive diplomacy. You’ll learn the agenda and meet your committee.

committeeSessions.title: Committee Sessions
committeeSessions.body: Delegates debate topics, deliver speeches, and work in blocs to draft working papers and resolutions. This is where the real diplomacy happens.

unmoderatedCaucuses.title: Unmoderated Caucuses
unmoderatedCaucuses.body: Informal negotiation time where delegates move freely, form alliances, and draft collaborative documents away from the formal setting.

votingAndAwards.title: Voting & Awards
votingAndAwards.body: Committees vote on resolutions, and outstanding delegates are recognized with awards like Best Delegate, Outstanding Delegate, and Honorable Mention.

conferenceSpread.heading: Low-cost conferences designed for every student.
conferenceSpread.description: We believe financial barriers should never prevent a student from experiencing Model UN. JAMUN conferences are affordable by design, and grants are available to cover up to 100% of conference costs for those who need them.

skills.heading: Skills that last a lifetime.
skills.subtitle: Model UN isn’t just about debating — it’s about building skills that matter in school, college applications, and beyond.

publicSpeaking.title: Public Speaking
publicSpeaking.description: Gain confidence speaking in front of groups, structuring arguments, and thinking on your feet under pressure.

criticalThinking.title: Critical Thinking

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions.
faq.subtitle: Everything parents and students want to know about JAMUN Model UN.

experience.q: Do I need any experience to join Model UN?
experience.a: Not at all! Our program is designed specifically for beginners. We’ll teach you everything from how to research your country to parliamentary procedure. Many of our most successful delegates started with zero experience.

grades.a: JAMUN Model UN is designed for middle school students in grades 5–8. We create age-appropriate committees and topics that challenge students while remaining accessible.

time.q: How much time does Model UN require?
time.a: Typically, clubs meet weekly for 1–2 hours. Before conferences, delegates spend additional time researching their country and preparing speeches. Conferences are usually 1–2 days on weekends.

topics.a: Topics range from climate change and human rights to technology regulation and international security. We select topics that are relevant, engaging, and appropriate for middle schoolers to explore.

cost.q: Is there a cost to participate?
cost.a: We work hard to keep costs minimal. Many of our resources are FREE, and we offer financial assistance for conference registration fees. No student should be unable to participate due to cost.

awards.a: Delegates can earn awards like Best Delegate, Outstanding Delegate, and Honorable Mention based on their preparation, speaking skills, collaboration, and adherence to their country’s positions.

finalCta.description: Join hundreds of middle schoolers who are building confidence, sharpening skills, and making friends through Model UN. No experience needed — just curiosity and a willingness to learn.

PrivacyPage.description: Learn how JAMUN protects your privacy and handles your personal information.
PrivacyPage.introText: The Junior Assembly of Model United Nations ("JAMUN," "we," "us," or "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website jamun.org and participate in our programs.
PrivacyPage.personalInfoText: We may collect personal information that you voluntarily provide to us when you:
PrivacyPage.personalInfoItem1: Register for our programs (Model UN, Mock Trial, or Mathletes)
PrivacyPage.personalInfoItem5: Apply for grants or volunteer positions

PrivacyPage.infoItem4: Payment information (processed securely through third-party providers)
PrivacyPage.autoCollectText: When you visit our website, we may automatically collect certain information about your device, including your IP address, browser type, operating system, and browsing behavior. We use Google Analytics to help us understand how visitors use our site.

We collect information about minor participants only with parental or guardian consent and use it solely for program administration. Parents and guardians may contact us at any time to review, update, or delete their child's information.
PrivacyPage.photoVideoText1: JAMUN may take photographs and video recordings of participants at our events, conferences, and programs. These images and videos may be used for promotional and educational purposes, including but not limited to:

PrivacyPage.photoVideoText3: prior to the event. We will make reasonable efforts to accommodate such requests, though we cannot guarantee complete exclusion from group photos or wide-angle shots.

ProgramsPage.title: Programs
ProgramsPage.description: Explore JAMUN's academic competition programs: Model UN, Mock Trial, and Mathletes for middle school students grades 5-8.
ProgramsPage.heroSubtitle: Whether you love debating global issues in Model UN, arguing cases in Mock Trial court, or solving challenging math problems in Mathletes competitions—we have a program that will ignite your passion and build skills for high school, college, and beyond. All designed specifically for middle school students in grades 5-8, all beginner-friendly.
ProgramsPage.statsTitle: Academic Competitions Built for Middle School Students
ProgramsPage.statsSubtitle: Every program is designed specifically for grades 5-8, with no prior experience required. Build public speaking, critical thinking, and leadership skills that prepare you for magnet schools, high school honors tracks, and college admissions.
ProgramsPage.modelUNDescription: Step into the shoes of UN delegates and tackle real-world global issues. Model UN develops critical thinking, public speaking, and negotiation skills while building deep understanding of international relations.
ProgramsPage.modelUNLongDescription: Model United Nations simulates the United Nations where students represent different countries and debate global issues. Delegates research their assigned country's positions, write position papers, give speeches, and work with others to draft resolutions addressing world challenges like climate change, human rights, and international security.
ProgramsPage.modelUNFeature1Description: Deliver speeches, respond to questions, and articulate your country's position clearly.
ProgramsPage.modelUNFeature2Description: Work with other delegates to build coalitions and draft consensus resolutions.
ProgramsPage.modelUNFeature3Description: Learn to research complex topics and understand multiple perspectives on global issues.
ProgramsPage.modelUNFeature4Description: Practice diplomatic negotiation, compromise, and persuasion in a professional setting.
ProgramsPage.modelUNLearn1: How the United Nations and international diplomacy work
ProgramsPage.modelUNLearn2: Research and analysis of complex global issues
ProgramsPage.modelUNLearn3: Public speaking and formal debate techniques
ProgramsPage.modelUNLearn4: Writing position papers and draft resolutions
ProgramsPage.modelUNTestimonialQuote: Model UN taught me that I could speak up and be heard. It changed how I see myself and my ability to make a difference.
ProgramsPage.mockTrialDescription: Experience the drama of the courtroom as attorneys and witnesses bring cases to life. Mock Trial develops argumentation, critical analysis, and presentation skills that translate to success in any field.
ProgramsPage.mockTrialLongDescription: Mock Trial is a competitive simulation where students take on the roles of attorneys and witnesses to argue a fictional legal case. Teams prepare both prosecution/plaintiff and defense sides, learning to craft opening statements, conduct direct and cross-examinations, and deliver closing arguments before real judges.
ProgramsPage.mockTrialFeature1Description: Learn to construct logical arguments, analyze evidence, and think like a lawyer.
ProgramsPage.mockTrialFeature2Description: Dissect complex cases, identify key facts, and develop winning strategies.
ProgramsPage.mockTrialFeature3Description: Master the art of questioning witnesses through direct and cross-examination.
ProgramsPage.mockTrialFeature4Description: Bring witnesses to life and deliver compelling arguments that persuade judges.
ProgramsPage.mockTrialLearn1: Fundamentals of the American legal system
ProgramsPage.mockTrialLearn2: How to construct and refute legal arguments
ProgramsPage.mockTrialTestimonialQuote: Mock Trial pushed me out of my comfort zone. Now I can think on my feet and argue my point confidently.
ProgramsPage.mockTrialTestimonialAuthor: Dustin Simon, Director of Mock Trial
ProgramsPage.mathletesDescription: Challenge yourself with competitive mathematics and discover the thrill of problem-solving. Mathletes builds analytical thinking, perseverance, and teamwork through engaging math competitions.
ProgramsPage.mathletesLongDescription: Mathletes is a competitive mathematics program where students tackle challenging problems ranging from arithmetic and algebra to geometry and number theory. Students compete individually and as teams, developing speed, accuracy, and creative problem-solving approaches while building a supportive math community.
ProgramsPage.mathletesFeature1Description: Develop creative approaches to challenging problems beyond standard curriculum.
ProgramsPage.mathletesFeature2Description: Build computational fluency and confidence in time-pressured situations.
ProgramsPage.mathletesFeature3Description: Collaborate on relay rounds and team challenges that require coordination.
ProgramsPage.mathletesFeature4Description: Earn awards and recognition for mathematical achievement at competitions.
ProgramsPage.mathletesTestimonialQuote: Mathematics isn't just about numbers—it's about developing the problem-solving mindset that unlocks every door in life.
ProgramsPage.whyChooseSubtitle: Every program is designed with middle schoolers in mind—accessible, supportive, and genuinely fun.
ProgramsPage.whyChooseBeginnerDescription: No experience needed. We teach everything from the ground up with patient, supportive mentors.
ProgramsPage.whyChooseCompetitionsDescription: Compete in actual tournaments and conferences, earning recognition and building your resume.
ProgramsPage.whyChooseResourcesDescription: Access training materials, practice problems, and curriculum guides at no cost.
ProgramsPage.whyChooseSkillsDescription: Build transferable skills in communication, critical thinking, and teamwork.
ProgramsPage.ctaSubtitle: Whether you're a student eager to compete or a teacher looking to start a program at your school, we're here to help you take the first step.
ProgramsPage.pickYourPathDescription: Three programs. One mission. Every program is beginner-friendly and built specifically for grades 5–8.

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before signing up.

whoShouldFill.q: Who should fill out this registration form?
whoShouldFill.a: An adult advisor — either a school employee sponsoring a delegation or an adult (21+) sponsoring an independent student. Students cannot register themselves.

registrationFee.a: The registration fee is $15 per delegate, due before the conference deadline. This covers all conference materials, committee placements, and event logistics.

multipleSchools.q: Can I register students from multiple schools?
multipleSchools.a: Each registration covers one school or independent group. If you’re advising delegations from multiple schools, please submit a separate registration for each.

unknownCount.q: What if I don’t know exactly how many students yet?
unknownCount.a: Enter your best estimate. You can update the number later by emailing us at contact@jamun.org before the registration deadline.

priorExperience.q: Do my students need prior experience?
priorExperience.a: Not at all. JAMUN conferences are beginner-friendly. When you note your students’ experience level, we use it to match them with appropriate committees — not to exclude anyone.

financialAssistance.a: Yes. JAMUN offers grants covering up to 100% of conference costs. Visit our grants page to apply. Grant applications are reviewed separately from registration.

ModelUNHomePage.faq1Question: Do I need any experience to join Model UN?

ModelUNHomePage.faq2Answer: JAMUN Model UN is designed for middle school students in grades 5-8. We create age-appropriate committees and topics that challenge students while remaining accessible.
ModelUNHomePage.faq3Question: How much time does Model UN require?
ModelUNHomePage.faq3Answer: Typically, clubs meet weekly for 1-2 hours. Before conferences, delegates spend additional time researching their country and preparing speeches. Conferences are usually 1-2 days on weekends.
ModelUNHomePage.faq4Answer: Topics range from climate change and human rights to technology regulation and international security. We select topics that are relevant, engaging, and appropriate for middle schoolers to explore.

MockTrialHomePage.faq2Answer: JAMUN Mock Trial is designed for middle school students in grades 5-8. We create age-appropriate cases and provide training that meets students where they are.
MockTrialHomePage.faq3Answer: Mock Trial launches in Fall 2026! We are currently building our curriculum and case materials. Join our mailing list to be the first to know when registration opens.
MockTrialHomePage.faq4Answer: You can play an attorney (prosecution or defense) or a witness. Most students get the opportunity to try both roles throughout the season, so you can find what suits you best.
MockTrialHomePage.faq5Question: How much time does Mock Trial require?
MockTrialHomePage.faq5Answer: Mock Trial involves about 5 hours per week with team practices and individual preparation. Competitions are typically full-day events on weekends, with 2 competitions per year.
MockTrialHomePage.faq6Question: Is there a cost to participate?
MockTrialHomePage.faq6Answer: We work hard to keep costs minimal. Many of our resources are FREE, and we offer financial assistance for competition fees. No student should be unable to participate due to cost.

MathletesHomePage.faq1Question: Do I need to be a math genius to join Mathletes?
MathletesHomePage.faq1Answer: Not at all! Mathletes is for students who enjoy math and want to challenge themselves. We welcome all skill levels and provide training that helps everyone improve. Some of our best competitors started with no competition experience.
MathletesHomePage.faq2Answer: JAMUN Mathletes is designed for middle school students in grades 5-8. We create age-appropriate practice materials and competition divisions so students compete with peers at similar levels.
MathletesHomePage.faq3Answer: Mathletes begins in Fall 2026! We are currently building our curriculum and partnerships. Join our mailing list to be the first to know when registration opens.
MathletesHomePage.faq4Question: How much time does Mathletes require?
MathletesHomePage.faq4Answer: About 4 hours per week with meetings every two weeks and 4 tournaments per year. Students are encouraged to do additional individual practice, but the time commitment is flexible based on your goals.
MathletesHomePage.faq5Question: What math level do I need?
MathletesHomePage.faq5Answer: If you are comfortable with pre-algebra concepts, you are ready to start! Our training covers topics from basic arithmetic through introductory algebra and geometry.
MathletesHomePage.faq6Question: Is there a cost to participate?
MathletesHomePage.faq6Answer: We work hard to keep costs minimal. Many of our resources are FREE, and we offer financial assistance for competition registration fees. No student should be unable to participate due to cost.
